guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: A deterministic suite covers first outing, station loop, routes, nursery touch, Root Hollow, Season Threads, High Pass, filing, and filed return.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Assemble seeded save/test helpers and ensure station/map/journal state can be asserted without brittle UI timing assumptions.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_338_target: Add a test-only runtime surface matrix for the six debug save snapshots so full-arc station/map/journal states can be asserted through render_game_to_text without browser traversal.
systems_targets: boot each debug save snapshot through createGame and fake DOM helpers
assert first-session starter request and beach route-board target
assert station-return world-map to field-station default flow and station-return beat state
assert season-close-return station open clears the pending close and exposes the next field-season High Pass shell
assert high-pass-active journal/map/station surfaces point at treeline-high-pass without walking the route
assert high-pass-ready-to-file station route board exposes treeline-high-pass notebookReady state
assert high-pass-filed station and journal surfaces are settled with no active outing/request
non_goals: Route v2 route-state progression matrix duplication
filed-note or copy-budget matrix duplication
screenshot manifest or browser proof work
new save snapshots unless a real gap is found
save schema or migration changes
player-facing copy, station redesign, tutorial UI, telemetry, geometry, or content additions
fixing current src/test/field-requests.test.ts build blockers from another lane
verification: npm run build attempted only to record whether the current field-requests.test.ts blocker remains

runtime_matrix: first-session starter request and beach route-board target
station-return menu-to-map-to-field-station defaults and active station-return beat
season-close-return station open into next-season High Pass routes shell and treeline map focus
high-pass-active active request, journal card, route board, world-map focus, and Rimed Pass replay label
high-pass-ready-to-file active request and High Pass filing route-board state
high-pass-filed settled station, route board, and journal state with no active outing/request
notes: The scout shorthand called out notebookReady for High Pass ready-to-file; the live High Pass route-board surface represents that state through the existing launchCard progressLabel NOTE plus ready-to-file nextDirection instead of a notebookReady beat property.
The prior field-requests TypeScript build blocker is no longer present in this worktree; full build passed.

verified: test-only matrix uses existing debug save snapshots and fake DOM helpers
six lane-1 snapshot states cover station, map, journal, active request, ready-to-file, and filed High Pass surfaces
lane-1 matrix does not duplicate lane-2 filed-note copy, lane-3 screenshot proof, or lane-4 route-state progression work

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Assert key filed-note and synthesis strings stay under budget and retain the intended ecology relationship in each arc segment.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_339_target: Add a lane-2, test-only filed-note synthesis matrix for every live Route v2 filed-note route in the completed alpha arc.
copy_contract: assert all live Route v2 note ids are present and no new route-note id appears without choosing a budget/anchor
protect routeV2Note readyText, filedText, clueBackedTail, and displayPrefix budgets in content-quality coverage
add a table-driven resolved-note matrix that checks filed-note and display-text lengths, relationship anchors, and the display-only Thaw Window prefix
use a current-safe generated filed-note ceiling near 144 characters unless implementation deliberately tightens copy first
non_goals: route progression, route controller behavior, support behavior, station filing, map travel, save schema, or geometry changes
new discoveries, new facts, new route objectives, broad ecosystem-note prose, or player-facing UI
duplicating lane 4's route-state progression matrix or lane 1's seeded save snapshot work
verification: `npm run build` if TypeScript test edits need a compiler-wide check

main_result.summary: Added test-only Route v2 note-copy and resolved filed/display synthesis matrices across the 11 completed-alpha route-note ids.
boundaries_kept: no route progression, route controller behavior, support behavior, station filing, map travel, save schema, geometry, new discoveries, new facts, or player-facing UI changes
kept the lane-2 pass limited to content/copy budgets and relationship anchors
verification: `npm run build` attempted but currently blocked by unrelated in-progress lane edits documented in the implementation report

critic_result.summary: No lane-2 blocker found; the filed-note matrix stays test-only, content-facing, and bounded to copy budgets plus ecology anchors.
verification: `npm run build` remains blocked by a lane-4 route-state matrix type mismatch outside this lane-2 change

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Attach browser/screenshot proof points for the spatial endpoints used in the smoke matrix without adding new places.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_340_target: Bridge the screenshot-proof manifest to packet 133 by naming full-arc spatial checkpoints and crediting existing forest frame state JSON, without generating screenshots or adding places.
spatial_checkpoints: Tundra process support -> tundra-drift-hold and tundra-thaw-bench
non_goals: new places, platforms, inspectables, carriers, terrain, climbables, or biome geometry
route behavior, route state, support behavior, station/menu behavior, save snapshots, save schema, science copy, journal copy, or player-facing UI
committed screenshot output, committed state dumps, committed browser-error JSON, or a new screenshot automation framework
duplicating lane 1 debug save snapshots or lane 4 route-state matrix assertions
verification: check updated manifest reference paths exist locally
runtime tests and `npm run build` only if runtime or test code changes

main_result.summary: Added the manifest bridge from packet 133's full-arc smoke matrix to existing spatial proof frames and credited the existing forest frame state JSON.
boundaries_kept: no geometry, route behavior, route state, support behavior, station/menu behavior, save snapshots, save schema, science copy, journal copy, or player-facing UI changes
no committed screenshots, state dumps, browser-error JSON, or screenshot automation framework
verification: checked referenced existing forest state paths locally

critic_result.summary: The manifest bridge stays docs-only, maps the required full-arc spatial checkpoints to existing frame ids and ignored proof targets, and leaves route-state, save, UI, geometry, and committed-output ownership untouched.
watch_items: If later agents turn more non-forest rows into strict state baselines, add those state JSON paths to the quick path-check snippet at the same time.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Build the route-state progression matrix across all live Route v2 outings, supports, active windows, and post-filed states.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_341_target: Add one compact deterministic Route v2 matrix in field-request tests that walks every live notebook route from active state through clue progress, ready-to-file, filing, and next-state handoff.
matrix_assertions: active route id, title, progress label, routeV2 status, selected support id, and starting evidence state
expected slot ids and entry ids as each clue is gathered, including ordered gates for ordered routes
ready-to-synthesize result, NOTEBOOK READY notice, Ready To File active state, and clue-backed filed text where applicable
fileReadyRouteV2FieldRequest clears routeV2Progress, records the completed request id, and hands off to the next request or final filed state
process/world-state variants stay live-only while canonical ready/filed identity remains stable
non_goals: new route framework
station page or station layout changes
browser automation flow
committed screenshots or state dumps
save schema changes
science copy changes
broad onboarding copy changes
geometry or biome placement changes
duplicating lane 1 debug save snapshots
duplicating lane 3 screenshot proof
verification: `npm run build` only if runtime TypeScript changes

main_result.summary: Added a test-only, table-driven Route v2 matrix across all 11 live notebook routes, including clue progression, ready-to-file state, filing handoffs, and live-only process/world-state variants.
boundaries_kept: no runtime route behavior, save schema, station page, browser automation, screenshot output, science/copy rewrite, geometry, new route framework, lane-1 save snapshot duplication, or lane-3 screenshot-proof duplication
kept forest-survey-slice, coastal-edge-moisture, tundra-survey-slice, and forest-season-threads as handoff-only states

critic_result.summary: The lane-4 route-state matrix covers all 11 live Route v2 notebook routes, keeps handoff-only ids out of the route list, preserves canonical ready/filed identity for live-only variants, and stays test-only.
watch_items: Future Route v2 additions should add a route-state matrix row alongside the route definition.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.
